is a [combat sport](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport") exercise in which a person throws punches at the air as though there is an opponent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Practised primarily in [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"), it is used mainly to prepare the muscles before the person training engages in stronger physical activity.[[2]](#cite_note-2) [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali "Muhammad Ali") once [performed a now famous shadowboxing routine](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfJTi-WAmxg) next to [Howard Cosell](/wiki/Howard_Cosell "Howard Cosell") for [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company")'s *[Wide World of Sports](/wiki/Wide_World_of_Sports_(US_TV_series) "Wide World of Sports (US TV series)")* [television](/wiki/Television "Television") [cameras](/wiki/Camera "Camera"). [Black Nova Scotian](/wiki/Black_Nova_Scotians "Black Nova Scotians") boxer [George Dixon](/wiki/George_Dixon_(boxer) "George Dixon (boxer)") is widely credited for developing the technique.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Sports training exercise

For other uses, see [Shadowboxing (disambiguation)](/wiki/Shadowboxing_(disambiguation) "Shadowboxing (disambiguation)").

"Sciamachy" redirects here. For the satellite spectrometer on *ENVISAT*, see [SCIAMACHY](/wiki/SCIAMACHY "SCIAMACHY").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Shadowboxing "Special:EditPage/Shadowboxing") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Shadowboxing"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Shadowboxing%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Shadowboxing%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Shadowboxing%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Shadowboxing%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Shadowboxing%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Shadowboxing%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2011)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Jab3.jpg/250px-Jab3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jab3.jpg)

*Shadowboxing*

**Shadowboxing** is a [combat sport](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport") exercise in which a person throws punches at the air as though there is an opponent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Practised primarily in [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"), it is used mainly to prepare the muscles before the person training engages in stronger physical activity.[[2]](#cite_note-2) [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali "Muhammad Ali") once [performed a now famous shadowboxing routine](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfJTi-WAmxg) next to [Howard Cosell](/wiki/Howard_Cosell "Howard Cosell") for [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company")'s *[Wide World of Sports](/wiki/Wide_World_of_Sports_(US_TV_series) "Wide World of Sports (US TV series)")* [television](/wiki/Television "Television") [cameras](/wiki/Camera "Camera"). [Black Nova Scotian](/wiki/Black_Nova_Scotians "Black Nova Scotians") boxer [George Dixon](/wiki/George_Dixon_(boxer) "George Dixon (boxer)") is widely credited for developing the technique.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Details

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shadowboxing&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Details")]

Most [boxing trainers](/wiki/Boxing_training "Boxing training") prefer that their fighters do their shadow boxing before engaging in any other daily exercise routines. The main purpose of this exercise, apart from getting the muscles ready for another activity, is usually to maintain a fighter's rhythm and show the fighter how they would look at that stage of training against a certain opponent. This could be important as fighters envision themselves facing their immediate future opponents: it usually gives fighters an idea of what is, and what is not, to be fixed.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It is also beneficial to sometimes incorporate dumbbells as you shadow box, so punches can flow quicker without them. It is also good to look in a mirror as you shadowbox so you can catch slight errors in your rhythm.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Fighters may want to do some shadowboxing of their own after their daily routines are over, either inside a boxing gym's [ring](/wiki/Boxing_ring "Boxing ring"), or wherever they please to at home without having to look directly at a mirror.

Each boxing round is 3 minutes. Shadowboxing is all about flowing offensively and defensively and it is also important to not throw full punches all the time, it can drain an individual out before the actual training starts.

Shadowboxing is not limited to boxers and fighters. Many fighters from other striking-based [martial arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts") also use the exercise as part of their daily routines and aside from punches and perhaps dependent upon fighting style they will use kicks, knees, elbows, or even throws. [Bruce Lee](/wiki/Bruce_Lee "Bruce Lee") was often seen practicing his kicks in front of a mirror in his films, and he incorporated other concepts from boxing into his [Jeet Kune Do](/wiki/Jeet_Kune_Do "Jeet Kune Do") style such as footwork and [live sparring](/wiki/Sparring "Sparring"). Shadowboxing with swords and other weapons, referred to as a floryshe, is a major training tool among [Historical European Martial Arts groups](/wiki/Historical_European_martial_arts "Historical European martial arts") such as the [Association for Renaissance Martial Arts](/wiki/Association_for_Renaissance_Martial_Arts "Association for Renaissance Martial Arts") and the HEMA Alliance.

Fighters of other more grappling-based martial arts also shadowbox as part of their daily training regimen. [Freestyle wrestlers](/wiki/Freestyle_wrestling "Freestyle wrestling") also practice many drills simulating specific wrestling moves without the aid of a partner such as shooting, sprawling, hip switches and bridging.

### Styles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shadowboxing&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Styles")]

The *long* method involves a shuffle of the feet that rocks the body back and forth. This is a style favored by fighters with long reach, who use more jabs and straight shots.
The *short* method sees the fighter move his head and body to the left and right, constantly slipping punches and moving in for closer body shots. [Joe Frazier](/wiki/Joe_Frazier "Joe Frazier") and [Mike Tyson](/wiki/Mike_Tyson "Mike Tyson") are among the best examples of fighters who use this method.

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shadowboxing&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

In 2024, baseball player [Tommy Pham](/wiki/Tommy_Pham "Tommy Pham") of the [Chicago White Sox](/wiki/Chicago_White_Sox "Chicago White Sox") shadowboxed after an altercation with [William Contreras](/wiki/William_Contreras "William Contreras") of the [Milwaukee Brewers](/wiki/Milwaukee_Brewers "Milwaukee Brewers").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
